what_it_does: A Round 2 research findings report that audits the guided-turn injection path and proposes a runtime-tunable cast-posture configuration axis, mapping what is observed-complete, partial, and still needed.
observations: The canonical INJECTION_RULE path from rule fire through chat_parts to _chat_part_core is traced end-to-end and observed as complete for recall and ground.
_chat_part_core consumes the inject argument by appending a [CONTEXT recalled this turn ...] block to Part 2 user content (suite.py:5075-5077).
inject_text is a scalar string assigned per firing rule, so multiple destination=inject rules would overwrite each other rather than accumulate.
connect, check, and voice roles carry descriptive {id, reads, effect, kind} rule entries that are explicitly skipped at suite.py:5397-5399; their outputs are stored but not consumed.
chat_parts only acts on destination==inject rules; route() in rules.py handles chain/address/surface/lane but these are not consumed during live part assembly.
K1 is framed as six mode_scope edits that would make recall and ground inject immediately with no further code changes.
A cast_posture axis is proposed: default enriched in MODE_REGISTRY, runtime-tunable via rhm_config, read once at the fireable filter in chat_parts; lean=recall+ground only, off=empty cast.
Per-subtype posture override is flagged as net-new wiring because cast_for_mode currently keys only on top-level mode.
standouts: Injection edge is observed complete for recall+ground but only because a single canonical rule lands in a single scalar slot.
connect/check/voice outputs are present in the store yet dormant, awaiting AST-shaped inject rules.
The report packages a concrete config-axis design and a ready completion criterion for cast posture.
conventions: Uses a status table and an explicit config criterion section.
Maintains a neutral, file-local tone without ranking alternatives.
concerns: Phrases like 80% of an enriched guided turn mix inferred judgment with observed facts.
K1, G3, and G4 are referenced as external milestones without being defined in the file.
The overwrite-bound is framed as a future limit, but the report does not quantify how likely multiple inject rules are.
notes: A build-prep audit that says recall/ground injection is already wired, connect/check/voice are half-wired, and proposes a two-layer cast-posture knob.
questions: What exact AST rule shape and destination are intended for connect, check, and voice?
How should multiple inject values be accumulated into a coherent context block?
Is per-subtype cast_posture wiring a required deliverable or an optional future enhancement?
What is K1 in this repository, and where are the six mode_scope edits located?
How does cast_posture interact with existing MODE_AUTODETECT and brain_knobs runtime config?
purpose_vs_actual: Yes — the file's stated scope (targeted reads of the injection and config axis) matches the end-to-end path audit and config design it delivers.
apparent_themes: Declarative registry defaults layered with runtime config overrides.
intention_signals: It is trying to give the build team a clear, evidence-graded readiness map of guided-turn cognition injection and a concrete, runtime-tunable cast-posture design.
novelty: It explicitly flags the single scalar inject_text overwrite bound and packages the cast-posture axis as a ready completion criterion.
contribution: The end-to-end injection-path observation, the partial-state inventory for connect/check/voice, and the two-layer cast-posture config design with a completion criterion.
summary_for_embedding: A Round 2 findings report auditing the guided-turn chat_parts injection path, declaring recall/ground injection complete, connect/check/voice dormant pending AST rules, and proposing a MODE_REGISTRY plus rhm_config cast_posture axis.
intention_for_embedding: To inform build planning by separating already-wired injection behavior from remaining config and rule-authoring work.
